args.Description: "Fix classic React JSX bug: `count > 0 && <Chip>` renders literal '0' when count is 0 because 0 is falsy but React renders numbers. Changed to ternary `count > 0 ? <Chip> : null`.\""
args.Instruction: "Fix 0-rendering bug with ternary"
args.ReplacementContent: "              count > 0 ? <Chip label={`${count} ${t('argus.settings.configured')}`} size=\"small\"\n                sx={{ height: 18, fontSize: '0.58rem', fontWeight: 600, backgroundColor: alpha('#4caf50', 0.1), color: '#4caf50', border: 'none', mt: 0.3 }} /> : null"
args.TargetContent: "              count > 0 && <Chip label={`${count} ${t('argus.settings.configured')}`} size=\"small\"\n                sx={{ height: 18, fontSize: '0.58rem', fontWeight: 600, backgroundColor: alpha('#4caf50', 0.1), color: '#4caf50', border: 'none', mt: 0.3 }} />"
